model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size raises examples seen per run, which often improves early validation loss on CIFAR-10. A modest LR increase keeps update scale aligned with the larger batch while staying simple and low-risk.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, scheduling LR across exactly the available optimization steps can improve final validation loss versus a constant LR. This keeps the current successful optimizer/batch setup and adds a minimal, budget-aligned cosine decay.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The cosine schedule degraded this workload, so reverting to constant LR restores the stronger regime. Switching from Adam to AdamW keeps optimization similar while using decoupled weight decay, which can improve early-budget generalization without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With AdamW and a short fixed-step budget, the current setup may still be slightly under-updating. A moderate LR increase is a simple high-leverage probe for better loss within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher LR regressed, so restoring 1e-3 returns to the stronger update scale. Then a modestly lower decoupled weight decay may improve early-budget fitting while keeping regularization.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"A small increase in classifier head capacity can improve class separation with minimal complexity and limited runtime impact, while keeping the successful optimizer/batch regime unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Revert the head-width increase that hurt loss, then add light label smoothing to improve calibration and generalization under the short fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Label smoothing regressed strongly, so revert to plain CE and restore the stronger weight decay setting. Then test a shorter AdamW second-moment horizon (beta2=0.99), which can improve progress in very short fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After improving optimizer dynamics with beta2=0.99, a small LR increase is a low-complexity local search around the current best point to potentially improve short-horizon convergence.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Restore the better LR, then test a larger batch to expose more unique examples within the same 256-step budget while keeping the successful AdamW(beta2=0.99) setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"insert_after","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"            loss.backward()","text":"\n            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger batch regressed, so revert to 128. Then add light gradient clipping as a low-complexity stability tweak that can help short-budget AdamW training avoid harmful large updates.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"delete_exact","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)\n","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Gradient clipping degraded validation loss in this workload, so removing it restores the better short-budget optimization behavior while keeping the strongest AdamW configuration found so far.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Test a modest convolutional width increase to improve representation power while keeping depth and training dynamics unchanged; this is a targeted capacity probe with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The width increase regressed, so revert to the previously better channel size to return to the strongest known architecture before using remaining budget on finer optimization tweaks.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Run a small local LR decrease around the current best configuration to test whether slightly more conservative updates improve validation loss at the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore the better LR and test stronger decoupled regularization, which can reduce early overfitting and potentially improve validation loss at the fixed 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The higher weight decay regressed, so restore 1e-4 and use the remaining budget to test lower beta1 for potentially faster adaptation in the short 256-step regime.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta1 reduction regressed, so revert to the best-observed optimizer setting to finish on the strongest known configuration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
